name: gate
description: portitor's push-verification core: the checks a git pre-receive runs against an incoming push, inspecting the resulting objects/refs (not commands). Generic git-gateway logic with no beads/spex knowledge. NOTE: this module.json is a hand-authored SEED in spexmachina's format — identity hashes (requirement id/preq_id, component spec_node_id) and spec/.snapshot.json are generated when run through `spex` / the /spec skill; they are intentionally omitted here.

requirements.title: Reject pushes to the default branch
requirements.description: A ref update targeting refs/heads/<default> (push or deletion) is a violation. The default branch is taken from Config.DefaultBranch, else derived from the receiving repo's HEAD symref.

requirements.title: Require every new commit to be signed by an allowed signer
requirements.description: Each commit an update introduces (git rev-list old..new, or new --not --all on create) must have git signature verdict %G? == G against an allowed_signers file. None (N), bad (B), untrusted/not-in-allowed-signers (U), or error (E) is a violation.

requirements.title: Attribute each commit to a role by signing-key fingerprint
requirements.description: The signer key fingerprint (git %GF) maps to a role via Config.Roles (fingerprint -> role). Identity is a credential, not a label: a container holding only one role's key cannot sign as another role. Untrusted commits (verdict != G) are rejected before role evaluation.

requirements.title: Enforce role-gated content rules
requirements.description: A RoleRule {Name, PathGlob, AddedRegex, AllowedRoles} requires that any introduced commit whose diff to PathGlob adds a line matching AddedRegex be signed by a role in AllowedRoles, else a violation named Name. Generic mechanism; domain rules (e.g. 'only reviewer/owner may close a bead' on .beads/issues.jsonl) are supplied as external config, not built in.

requirements.title: Reject the whole push atomically with all violations
requirements.description: pre-receive runs once per push over git's object quarantine. portitor collects every violation across all refs/commits and, if any exist, exits non-zero printing a complete, actionable report to stderr (surfaced to the pusher as remote: lines); exits 0 only when there are zero violations.

requirements.title: Delegate cryptography to git
requirements.description: Signature verification is performed by `git verify-commit` against allowed_signers; portitor orchestrates and decides. Keeps the trust in git, not in portitor's own crypto.

requirements.title: Gate is a pure, isolation-testable function
requirements.description: Check(repoDir, updates, cfg) -> ([]Violation, error) shells out only to git for facts, so it is testable with ephemeral throwaway keys and a temp bare repo — no real credentials or hardware.

components.name: Gate
components.description: Check() evaluates ref updates against the policy and returns all violations. Owns default-branch derivation, new-commit enumeration, per-commit signature verification + fingerprint->role attribution via git, and role-gated content rules.

components.name: PreReceiveCommand
components.description: The `portitor pre-receive` subcommand: parses stdin ref updates, loads config from env (PORTITOR_DEFAULT_BRANCH, PORTITOR_ALLOWED_SIGNERS, GIT_DIR), runs Gate.Check, and maps the result to an exit code + stderr report.
